text: systemd timers are the modern replacement for cron, providing calendar and monotonic event scheduling with better logging, randomization, and dependency management. Timer unit triggers a matching service unit. `systemctl list-timers` shows all active timers.
Timer types: OnCalendar (specific times, like cron: `*-*-* 02:00:00` = daily 2AM), OnBootSec (after boot), OnUnitActiveSec (after service last ran).
Accuracy: `AccuracySec` trades precision for power saving (default 1min).
